title: Partition data so work is bounded and replayable
description: Partition tables by an ingestion or event date (or a stable key) so each run touches one slice and backfills target single partitions.
body: Use when designing warehouse tables or pipeline write scope. Do not reprocess or reload the full table on every run — it does not scale, bloats cost, and makes a rerun overwrite good data with whatever the current source returns. Actions: choose a partition column (usually event/ingestion date), write one partition per run, and enable partition pruning in queries. Validation signals include queries that scan only the needed partitions and a backfill that rewrites exactly its target partition. Risk boundary: an unpartitioned full reload is expensive and unrecoverable on partial failure; treat full-table rewrites of large tables as needing review.
